title: OpenAI Just Bought a Popular Tech Podcast — and That Should Make You Curious
Anthropic Accidentally Nuked Thousands of Developers' Projects Chasing a Leak
Your AI Chatbot Has a Surprisingly Large Carbon Footprint — and It's Getting Bigger

notes: 😄 Someone on X just "vibe coded" an entire unicorn startup — meaning they described what they wanted to an AI in plain English and watched it build the thing, basically skipping the whole "knowing how to code" step. The post went viral because it's the clearest, most ridiculous proof yet that vibe coding (just vibing with AI until software appears) has crossed from meme into legitimate strategy. It's funny, a little terrifying, and says everything about where we are right now — when the barrier to building a company is basically just having good taste and a Wi-Fi connection.

🔥 Someone leaked Claude Code's entire source code online, and the internet did what the internet does best — cloned it immediately, with one developer even porting the whole thing to Python before Anthropic's legal team could finish typing their cease-and-desist. The post racked up 34 million views, which tells you everything about how hungry people are to peek behind the curtain of these AI tools they use every day. There's something deeply funny about an AI company's code going viral while the AI itself apparently tried to respond with legalese — the chaos is very much the point.
